text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers review each other's code before it is merged, they can catch bugs early in the process. This saves time and effort because fixing a problem later is much more expensive. In addition, code reviews encourage knowledge sharing. Team members learn different techniques, understand the project's codebase better, and can offer helpful suggestions. This process improves the overall quality of the code and helps build a stronger, more collaborative team. By working together, the team produces more reliable software.
